LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER MANZAR AZARI NOTICE OF APPLICATION AND PETITION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDAMUS AND REQUEST FOR STAY OF REVOCATION OF BROKER'S LICENSE IS TAKEN OFF CALENDAR PER AGREEMENT OF THE PARTIES.
OPPOSITION TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF ADMIN MANDAMUS FILED BY REAL PARTY IN INTEREST CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF REAL ESTATE

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER MANZAR AZARI'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDAMUS IS DENIED. MS. AZARI APPEARS TO CONCEDE THAT SHE VIOLATED NUMEROUS PROVISIONS OF THE STATUTES AND REGULATIONS IMPOSING MANDATORY REQUIREMENTS ON REAL ESTATE BROKER. EVEN IF MS. AZARI DID NOT SO CONCEDE, PER THE INDEPENDENT JUDGMENT STANDARD OF REVIEW, THE COMMISSIONER'S FINDINGS ARE AMPLY SUPPORTED BY THE WEIGHT OF THE EVIDENCE, ESPECIALLY SINCE THE GOVERNING LAW DOES NOT REQUIRE THAT THE VIOLATIONS BE INTENTIONALLY COMMITTED TO SUPPORT DISCIPLINARY ACTION. THE ONLY REAL ISSUE ON THIS WRIT IS WHETHER THE COMMISSIONER'S DECISION TO REVOKE MS. AZARI'S LICENSE CONSTITUTES AN ABUSE OF DISCRETION. BOTH SIDES RECOGNIZE THAT RELIEF IS AVAILABLE TO MS. AZARI ONLY WHERE ALL "REASONABLE MINDS" WOULD AGREE THAT A LESSER PENALTY RATHER THAN REVOCATION WAS APPROPRIATE GIVEN THE NATURE OF THE VIOLATIONS AND SURROUNDING CIRCUMSTANCES. HERE, WHILE REVOCATION OF MS. AZARI'S LICENSE IS ARGUABLY HARSH, IT CANNOT BE SAID THAT ALL REASONABLE MINDS WOULD AGREE THAT SOME LESSER PENALTY WAS MORE APPROPRIATE. THE VIOLATIONS GO TO THE HEART OF THE FIDUCIARY NATURE OF BEING A REAL ESTATE BROKER AND EVIDENCE, AT THE VERY LEAST, A TROUBLING LACK OF UNDERSTANDING OF BASIC FIDUCIARY PRINCIPLES. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Petition And Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus And Ntc Of Hearing And Lodging Of Administrative Record
Off Calendar Sep-18-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Steve C. Vondran, Esq. of the Law Office of Steve C. Vondran, Tel. 602.390.1967, for petitioner and moving party, by Court Call;
Joyce E. Hee, Esq., Deputy Attorney General, Tel. 510.622.2227, for respondent and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER MANZAR AZARI'S Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus is denied. Ms. Azari appears to concede that she violated numerous provisions of the statutes and regulations imposing mandatory requirements on real estate broker. Even if Ms. Azari did not so concede, per the independent judgment standard of review, the Commissioner's findings are amply supported by the weight of the evidence, especially since the governing law does not require that the violations be intentionally committed to support disciplinary action. The only real issue on this writ is whether the Commissioner's decision to revoke Ms. Azari's license constitutes an abuse of discretion. Both sides recognize that relief is available to Ms. Azari only where all "reasonable minds" would agree that a lesser penalty rather than revocation was appropriate given the nature of the violations and surrounding circumstances. Here, while revocation of Ms. Azari's license is arguably harsh, it cannot be said that all reasonable minds would agree that some lesser penalty was more appropriate. The violations go to the heart of the fiduciary nature of being a real estate broker and evidence, at the very least, a troubling lack of understanding of basic fiduciary principles. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Ntc Of Petition And Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus And Ntc Of Hearing And Lodging Of Administrative Record
Off Calendar Jul-17-2013 Continued To Sep-18-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. rULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling with modification. PETITIONER MANZAR AZARI's Hearing on Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus is continued to September 18, 2013 to give the parties a chance to comply with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. The opposing party needs to provide a courtesy copy of the opposition and the moving party needs to provide a courtesy copy of the reply papers. Please attach a cover letter to the papers reflecting the new hearing date. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
